get.description:
Public, unauthenticated liveness probe.

Used by the Lambda HTTP adapter (task 17.5) as a no-dependency smoke
route to confirm the ASGI app serves requests end-to-end.

post.description:
Issue ``count`` presigned PUT URLs (2-4, 5-min TTL) (Req 2.1, 2.4).

A count outside the 2-4 range raises ``InvalidPhotoCount`` -> 422; an unknown
item raises ``ItemNotFound`` -> 404.

Enqueue an async grading submission; return 202 + Idempotency-Key (Req 7.1, 7.2).

The Idempotency-Key is derived from the item id and the photo-set hash unless
an explicit override is supplied, then echoed in the ``Idempotency-Key``
response header. An unknown item is rejected with 404 before enqueueing.

get.description:
Return an Item's status plus exactly its persisted facets (Req 1.4).

An unknown identifier raises ``ItemNotFound`` -> 404 (Requirement 1.5).

post.description:
Run the routing decision for a graded Item (Requirement 10).

Persists the DECISION facet, emits a ROUTED lifecycle event identifying the
Item and its disposition, and returns the decision with its unit-economics
rationale (Requirement 10.7). An unknown item -> 404, an ungraded item ->
409.

Create the LISTING facet for a graded Item (Req 3.1, 3.5, 15.3).

An ungraded Item is rejected with 409 (grade required); an unknown Item with
404. On success the Item transitions to LISTED and a LISTED event is emitted.

get.description:
Return the LISTING facet for an Item (Requirement 3.3).

Unknown Item -> 404; an existing Item with no listing -> 404 (no listing).

put.description:
Apply a partial update to an existing listing (Requirement 3.2).

Unknown Item -> 404; an Item with no listing -> 404.

delete.description: Remove the LISTING facet for an Item (Requirement 3.4). Unknown Item -> 404.

post.description:
Simulated checkout for a listed Item (Requirements 15.5, 18.4).

Payments are simulated in v1: no payment is processed. The purchase requires
an existing listing (unknown Item or no listing -> 404), transitions the Item
to SOLD, and emits a SOLD lifecycle event identifying the Item.

get.description: Query listings for marketplace browse, enriched for buyers (Req 3.3, 13).

parameters.name: category

schema.description: Listing category to browse ('all' for every category)
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Listing category to browse ('all' for every category)

get.description:
Return ranked buyer matches for a graded Item (G1).

A 404 is raised for an unknown item (via the item-service lookup). An item
that exists but is not yet graded (no GRADE facet or status not GRADED)
returns an empty result: ``matches: []``, ``match_count_within_5km: 0``,
``top_reason: null``. The matching engine ranks candidate buyers and the view
is rendered with PII-free labels, distances, reasons, and intent tiers.
Return the prioritized review queue with server-computed tiers (G2).

post.description:
Confirm or override a pending grade; return the updated Item (G2).

CONFIRM persists the existing grade as confirmed; OVERRIDE builds a grade
from the item's current grade with the label replaced by ``override_grade``
(carrying confidence/summary/defects/completeness). Both advance the item to
GRADED. An action on a non-pending item -> 409; an invalid ``override_grade``
-> 422.

get.description:
Publicly verify a Product Health Card (Requirements 12.1-12.4, 16.3).

Recomputes the canonical HMAC-SHA256 signature and compares it against the
stored signature. Returns ``verified=true`` with the rendered card contents
on a match (Requirement 12.2) or ``verified=false`` with no contents when
tampered (Requirement 12.3). This route requires no authentication
(Requirements 12.4, 16.3). An unknown ``card_id`` yields a 404.

parameters.name: sig

schema.description: The signature presented on the public QR link
schema.title: Sig

parameters.description: The signature presented on the public QR link

message.title: Message
message.default: Simulated checkout: no payment processed. SOLD event emitted.

BuyResponse.title: BuyResponse
BuyResponse.description:
Simulated checkout result (Requirements 15.5, 18.4).

Payments are simulated in v1: no payment is processed. The purchase
transitions the Item to SOLD and a SOLD lifecycle event is emitted.

BuyerMatchModel.title: BuyerMatchModel
BuyerMatchModel.description:
A single ranked buyer match (G1).

``distance_km`` and ``match_score`` are JSON numbers; ``intent_tier`` is one
of ``HIGH``/``MEDIUM``/``LOW``; ``display_label`` is PII-free.

CardVerificationResponse.title: CardVerificationResponse
CardVerificationResponse.description:
Public verification outcome (Requirements 12.1-12.4, 16.3).

``verified`` is True when the recomputed signature matches the stored
signature, and ``card`` then carries the rendered card contents
(Requirement 12.2). When False the card is *tampered* and ``card`` is
``None`` so altered contents are never surfaced as authentic
(Requirement 12.3). ``reason`` is a short human-readable explanation.

CreateItemRequest.title: CreateItemRequest
CreateItemRequest.description:
Create-item body (Requirements 1.1, 1.2, 1.3).

All fields are optional at the transport layer so the *service* performs the
authoritative required-field validation and names the missing field in its
error (Requirement 1.3, mapped to 422). ``order_id`` is required only for an
order-scan context; the service enforces that per context shape.

CreateListingRequest.title: CreateListingRequest
CreateListingRequest.description:
Create-listing body (Requirements 3.1, 3.5).

Listing an Item requires it to be graded; the service rejects an ungraded
Item with a 409 grade-required error.

GradeAcceptedResponse.title: GradeAcceptedResponse
GradeAcceptedResponse.description: 202 Accepted body for an enqueued grading submission (Requirement 7.1).

GradeRequest.title: GradeRequest
GradeRequest.description:
Grade-submission body (Requirements 7.1, 7.2).

``photo_keys`` are the uploaded S3 object keys for the photo set; the
Idempotency-Key is derived from the item id and the photo-set hash unless an
explicit ``idempotency_key`` override is supplied.

HealthCardModel.title: HealthCardModel
HealthCardModel.description:
The verified contents of a Product Health Card (Requirements 11.1, 12.2).

Returned only when verification succeeds; the grade is carried as its
human-readable label and money/identifier fields are plain strings, so the
public, unauthenticated payload exposes exactly the rendered card contents.

ItemAggregateResponse.title: ItemAggregateResponse
ItemAggregateResponse.description: An Item's status plus exactly its persisted facets (Requirement 1.4).

ItemMetaResponse.title: ItemMetaResponse
ItemMetaResponse.description: The META facet of an Item returned on create and within an aggregate.

ListingResponse.title: ListingResponse
ListingResponse.description: A listing returned by listing CRUD (Requirement 3.x).

MarketListingModel.title: MarketListingModel

MarketplaceResponse.title: MarketplaceResponse
MarketplaceResponse.description: Marketplace query result (Requirement 3.3, 13; extended by G3/G4).

MatchesResponse.title: MatchesResponse
MatchesResponse.description:
Ranked buyer matches for a graded Item (G1).

``generated_at`` is the ISO-8601 instant the view was produced. An empty
result carries ``matches: []``, ``match_count_within_5km: 0``, and
``top_reason: null``.

PresignRequest.title: PresignRequest
PresignRequest.description:
Presigned-upload request (Requirement 2.1, 2.4).

``count`` is the number of photo slots; the service rejects counts outside
the inclusive 2-4 range with a 422 range error.

PresignResponse.title: PresignResponse
PresignResponse.description: The presigned upload targets for a grading submission.

PresignedUrlModel.title: PresignedUrlModel
PresignedUrlModel.description: A single presigned S3 PUT target (Requirement 2.1, 2.2).

ReviewActionRequest.title: ReviewActionRequest
ReviewActionRequest.description:
A reviewer confirm/override action (G2).

``action`` is ``CONFIRM`` or ``OVERRIDE``. When ``OVERRIDE``,
``override_grade`` must be a valid grade label; when ``CONFIRM`` it is
``null``.

ReviewQueueItemModel.title: ReviewQueueItemModel

RouteRequest.title: RouteRequest
RouteRequest.description:
Routing request (Requirement 10).

``geohash5`` optionally attaches a neighborhood demand signal to the
decision context; it never changes the margin-based selection.

UpdateListingRequest.title: UpdateListingRequest
UpdateListingRequest.description: Partial listing update (Requirement 3.2). Unset fields are unchanged.
